title: Redstone Signal Bridge (Diagonal Cross)
description: Crosses two redstone signals over each other in the same plane without interference, using a torch-based bridge. One signal travels east-west on the lower level; the other travels north-south on the upper level, crossing via a torch bridge so neither signal leaks into the other's path.

steps.title: East-west signal path (lower level)
steps.explanation: Stone base at y=0, z=1. Dust at (0,1,1), (1,1,1), (2,1,1) carries signal A from west to east along z=1. This is the horizontal signal running straight through.

steps.title: Bridge pillar
steps.explanation: Solid block at (1,2,1) — this is the bridge pillar. Signal B (north-south) will travel on TOP of this block while signal A travels underneath on y=1. The vertical separation keeps the two signals isolated.

steps.title: North-south signal path ramps
steps.explanation: Signal B enters from z=0 on a stone at (1,1,0). A dust at (1,1,0) starts the north ramp. It climbs to the bridge pillar at (1,2,1) via a stair-step: stone at (1,1,0) with dust on top, then dust on the pillar at (1,2,1). Similarly south ramp from pillar down to (1,1,2).
